questions.question: Explain the two phases in two-phase locking protocol and why the protocol is called 'two-phase'.
expectedPoints: Growing phase where locks are acquired but not released
Shrinking phase where locks are released but not acquired
The protocol ensures serializability by preventing transactions from acquiring new locks after releasing any lock

questions.question: Compare and contrast distributed two-phase locking with timestamp ordering protocol, highlighting their advantages, disadvantages, and suitable application scenarios.
expectedPoints: 2PL uses locking mechanisms with growing/shrinking phases
Timestamp ordering uses timestamps to order operations
2PL has higher communication overhead but lower abort rates
Timestamp ordering has lower overhead but higher abort rates
Timestamp ordering better when timestamps can be efficiently generated
Discussion of deadlock handling in both approaches
Suitability for different distributed system architectures
